item_text: the application initiates the process by requesting signals from the signal management system.
best_testcase_text: test case id: tc-signalmanagement-001 title: retrieve and display signals module: signal management source pages: 3 preconditions: signals are stored in the database. test data: - no specific input required. test steps: 1. application requests signals from the signal management system. 2. signal management system forwards the request to the data management system. 3. data management system queries the database for signals. 4. database returns the payload containing the signals. 5. data management system forwards the payload to the signal management system. 6. signal management system forwards the payload to the application. 7. application displays the list of signals. expected result: application displays the list of signals retrieved from the database.

item_text: the signal management system forwards the request to the data management system, which queries the database for signals.
best_testcase_text: test case id: tc-signalmanagement-001 title: retrieve and display signals module: signal management source pages: 3 preconditions: signals are stored in the database. test data: - no specific input required. test steps: 1. application requests signals from the signal management system. 2. signal management system forwards the request to the data management system. 3. data management system queries the database for signals. 4. database returns the payload containing the signals. 5. data management system forwards the payload to the signal management system. 6. signal management system forwards the payload to the application. 7. application displays the list of signals. expected result: application displays the list of signals retrieved from the database.

item_text: the database returns the payload to the data management system, which then forwards it back to the signal management system.
best_testcase_text: test case id: tc-signalmanagement-001 title: retrieve and display signals module: signal management source pages: 3 preconditions: signals are stored in the database. test data: - no specific input required. test steps: 1. application requests signals from the signal management system. 2. signal management system forwards the request to the data management system. 3. data management system queries the database for signals. 4. database returns the payload containing the signals. 5. data management system forwards the payload to the signal management system. 6. signal management system forwards the payload to the application. 7. application displays the list of signals. expected result: application displays the list of signals retrieved from the database.

item_text: finally, the signal management system displays either a null value or a list of signals to the application.

item_text: this diagram models the sequence of events during a login process. the user initiates the login by dispatching a request to the application, which then sends the credentials to the server for verification. the server checks the database for the provided credentials and compares them with the stored data. upon successful comparison, the server sends a confirmation back to the application, which then informs the user that the login was successful.

item_text: this sequence diagram illustrates the interaction between a user, an air quality activity, and a main activity within an application. the user starts the application, which then loads countries, retrieves states, regions, and cities. an intent is initiated to the main activity, which retrieves http data from the server and presents it to the user through a message handler. the diagram highlights the flow of data and control between these components, emphasizing the retrieval of geographical data and the handling of http requests.

item_text: this activity diagram models a device scanning process involving three main components: main, application, and database.
best_testcase_text: test case id: tc-scan-001 title: perform device scan module: scan source pages: 4, 5 preconditions: user is logged in and has at least one device. test data: device id: 12345 test steps: 1. user navigates to the device scan page. 2. user selects the device with id "12345". 3. user clicks on "start scan" button. 4. application sends a request to the database to perform the hardware scan. 5. database performs the hardware scan and provides the component name and impact. 6. application receives the scan results and returns them to the user. 7. application also returns the component name, impact, and device diagnostics. 8. user reviews the scan results. expected result: scan results are displayed to the user, including component name, impact, and device diagnostics.
